data.title: Canadian Domestic Rail Volumes Offset Weakness in U.S. — Market Talk
data.description:
0953 ET - While the volume of rail freight Canada receives from its nearest neighbor is down, domestic carloadings are helping counter that. Statistics Canada data shows the total volume of rail freight carried in Canada hit 30.2 million metric tons in August, up 4.5% in year and slightly above the five-year historical average of 30.1 million tons in August. The rise was driven by higher volumes of domestic loadings, both non-intermodal and intermodal. Freight coming in from the U.S. was down 7.1% at 3.4 million tons, as rail imports sank 13.9% and exports dropped 8.1% to the U.S. Of note, the data also shows the volume of canola moved by rail at 297,000 tons was the lowest in three years as the value of exports of the commodity sank 46.5% on-year after China imposed steep tariffs on Canadian canola. (robb.stewart@wsj.com; @RobbMStewart)

0941 ET - Inflation metrics are likely to improve in the near future, LPL Financial's Jeffrey Roach writes following September's mild CPI report. He says tariffs were likely the culprit for a steep 0.7% rise in apparel prices in September. Roach cites the Fed's Beige Book saying that some firms are eating up tariff-induced costs to preserve market share. Meanwhile, housing prices "are decelerating and should slow further amid the growing supply of multifamily housing and the shrinking demand from lower immigration," he says. Roach expects the Fed to continue easing throughout 2026. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

0937 ET - Before Rogers Communications can unlock the value from Maple Leaf Sports & Entertainment, it first needs to buy the remaining 25% owned by Kilmer Sports. Scotiabank's Maher Yaghi says in a report that the current estimate for the quarter-interest in MLSE is C$3.3 billion. Rogers would then need to sell a minority interest to private equity or launch an IPO to unlock upside, he says. Yaghi thinks this could happen in the second half of next year, and "if done successfully this could unlock around C$5 a share in the stock." (adriano.marchese@wsj.com)

0928 ET - September's inflation report "is a sigh of relief for the Fed and won't derail what is likely a second consecutive rate cut" next week, although inflation remains above the 2% target, Fitch's Olu Sonola writes. "As odd as it may seem, the Fed will be happy with inflation staying around 3% for the next couple of months." Sonola says the tariff passthrough "generally remains muted." Meanwhile, labor data are still missing, leaving markets to hope that the government shutdown will be over by the December monetary policy meeting and "the Fed has a clearer read on jobs," Sonola says. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

0927 ET - GSK won approval from the U.S. Food and Drug Administration for its blood-cancer drug Blenrep, securing a positive outcome in a decision that had been in doubt that is somewhat overshadowed by the medicine's limited label, UBS analysts say. The FDA approved Blenrep as a third line of treatments--for adult patients who had received at least two prior therapies--, instead of the second line GSK was hoping for, UBS says. Moreover, patients require an ophthalmic exam before every dose, which will come as a disappointment for GSK, according to UBS. "Whilst it is a positive for GSK that Blenrep will return to the U.S. market, the smaller indication and stringent [risk evaluation and mitigation strategy] likely reduces the commercial opportunity," the analysts say. Shares fall 1.1%. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0922 ET - Canada's competitiveness as an auto manufacturer is rapidly eroding, warns the Canadian Vehicle Manufacturers' Association. President Brian Kingston in a statement says his association supports Ottawa's goal of protecting the auto industry and its workers, but adds tariffs on Canadian vehicle manufacturers when they are already under pressure only makes a difficult situation worse. It comes after Canada moved to cut the number of vehicles Stellantis and General Motors can import tariff-free in response to company plans to reduce their manufacturing footprint in Canada. Kingston says that combined with legal threats and EV mandates, Canada should instead focus on strengthening the auto industry. (robb.stewart@wsj.com)

0914 ET - O'Reilly Automotive alluded to some upcoming weakness in the autoparts sector, but DA Davidson's Michael Baker says it's still a good business under the hood. The analyst notes that the stock dipped during O'Reilly's quarterly call due to comments on slowing transaction trends in their DIY business in some bigger ticket maintenance areas and on comments that the inflation-related benefits to comparatives may be peaking. Despite isolated softness in some DIY big-ticket categories, Baker says the overall business remains strong, with DIFM comparatives up over 10% and DIY holding steady in the low single digits. Baker says that O'Reilly's 4Q guidance implies a notable two-year acceleration, while historical patterns suggest post-earnings dips have consistently led to outperformance. (adriano.marchese@wsj.com)

0905 ET - A benign September inflation report keeps the Fed on pace to cut interest rates "at least for the next two meetings" of this year, Regan Capital's Skyler Weinand writes. Higher inflation would have made it harder for the central bank to ease monetary policy to support jobs. Weinand expects the Fed to take a pause "to review a few months of data before cutting again in 2026." He adds that the delayed September CPI, which was slightly cooler than expected, is "real and based on real data from September, however, the collection and reliability of the data continues to weaken." He expects alternative data sources to gain relevance going forward. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

0851 ET - Treasury yields decline and stock futures rise as the much-awaited U.S. September inflation report shows the 12-month pace accelerating to 3% from 2.9% as of August, and core slowing to 3% from 3.1%. Economists surveyed by WSJ expected 3.1% for both indicators. Futures markets massively price in a second 25-basis-point cut by the Fed Wednesday. As the government shutdown shows no signs of ending, the CPI report could be the only official indicator Wall Street will see for a while. The 10-year yield is at 3.976% and the two-year at 3.453%, both slightly lower than yesterday. Futures for the S$P 500 and Nasdaq both rise around 1%. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

0835 ET - The Food and Drug Administration's approval of GSK's Blenrep blood-cancer treatment marks a major milestone for the British pharma company, though it is unlikely to deliver an immediate boost to earnings, AJ Bell analyst Russ Mould writes. Blenrep could significantly grow sales in the U.S., where GSK is investing heavily in research and development as well as supply-chain infrastructure, Mould says. This should come as good news after a recent negative opinion on the drug from a U.S. advisory committee raised investor doubts over whether it would be approved, Mould writes. But the treatment's benefit to profit will take time to assess, Mould adds. Shares fall 1.5%. (william.gray@wsj.com)

0824 ET - Rogers Communications' new mobile subscribers are being onboarded at higher price points, suggesting the price war in the telecom sphere may be normalizing. In a TD Cowen report, Vince Valentini says pricing seems to have reached an inflection point and new gross adds for Rogers look to be coming in at higher prices than where they would have been six to nine months ago. "While this front book pricing may not yet exceed the average of back book pricing, we see a clear road map towards the stabilization in average revenue per unit in 2H/26 should wireless pricing continue to be rational," Valentini says. Still, he cautions that this isn't set in stone, with Black Friday promotions around the corner. (adriano.marchese@wsj.com)

0818 ET - West Fraser Timber is nimble enough to navigate whatever waters might come in the next few months. RBC's Matthew McKellar says in a report that West Fraser's low-cost focus, advantaged softwood lumber duty rate, geographical diversification, and strong balance sheet position it to withstand a variety of scenarios that could play out in wood products markets into 2026. Additionally, the analyst things that the company could also be a potential acquirer of high-quality assets that could come to market should a period of more challenging conditions continue.(adriano.marchese@wsj.com)

data.title: UK seizes record haul of illegal weight-loss drugs in factory raid
data.description:
By Sam Tabahriti

Britain's medicines regulator said on Friday it had made the world's largest seizure of unlicensed weight-loss medicines, dismantling a factory that made jabs labelled as containing the ingredient in Eli Lilly's Mounjaro.

Global pharmaceutical companies and regulators have raised alarms over a surge in counterfeit and unregulated weight-loss drugs, which are being trafficked globally and sold illegally online and through underground networks.

The Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA) said it had seized 2,000 injection pens labelled as containing tirzepatide - the ingredient in Eli Lilly's Mounjaro and Zepbound - and retatrutide, an experimental obesity drug also made by Eli Lilly, which is still in trials.

Tens of thousands of empty pens were also seized, along with raw chemicals.

Eli Lilly did not immediately respond to a request for comment.

FIRST FACTORY DISCOVERY IN UK

The agency said the factory in central England was the first of its kind discovered in Britain.

"This is a victory in the fight against the shameless criminals who are putting lives at risk by peddling dangerous and illegal weight loss jabs to make a quick buck," health minister Wes Streeting said in a statement.

"These unregulated products, made with no regard for safety or quality, posed a major risk to unwitting customers."

Both tirzepatide and retatrutide mimic hormones that suppress appetite and regulate blood sugar.

Britain's National Health Service prescribes weight-loss medicines only to patients who meet certain criteria. For those who obtain them privately, the drugs can cost hundreds of pounds per month.

In the United States, some people have begun sourcing raw ingredients online to self-manufacture GLP-1-based injections such as semaglutide - a drug developed by Novo Nordisk and used in Ozempic and Wegovy - citing high retail prices and limited access.

The trend has raised safety concerns among health officials, who warn that DIY formulations pose serious health risks due to contamination, incorrect dosing and lack of oversight.

($1 = 0.7451 pounds)

data.title: Illinois Tool Works Inc reports results for the quarter ended September 30 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Illinois Tool Works Inc reported quarterly adjusted earnings of $2.81​​ per share for the quarter ended September 30, higher than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of $2.65. The mean expectation of seventeen analysts for the quarter was for earnings of $2.71 per share. Wall Street expected results to range from $2.65 to $2.79 per share.

  • Revenue rose 2.3% to $4.06 billion from a year ago; analysts expected $4.08 billion.

  • Illinois Tool Works Inc's reported EPS for the quarter was $2.81​.

  • The company reported quarterly net income of $821 million.

  • Illinois Tool Works Inc shares had fallen by 1.3% this quarter and gained 1.5% so far this year.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had risen by about 1.3% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, two analysts negatively revised earnings estimates

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "hold" and the breakdown of recommendations is 2 "strong buy" or "buy," 11 "hold" and 6 "sell" or "strong sell." The average consensus recommendation for the consumer goods conglomerates peer group is "buy."

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Illinois Tool Works Inc is $260.50, about 1.2% above its last closing price of $257.44

This summary was machine generated from LSEG data October 24 at 01:53 p.m. UTC. ​All figures in US dollars unless otherwise stated. (For questions concerning the data in this report, contact Estimates.Support@lseg.com. For any other questions or feedback, contact RefinitivNewsSupport@thomsonreuters.com)

Brokerages cuts United Rentals PT on margin concerns
data.description:
** Brokerages trim PT on equipment rental firm United Rentals due to margin pressure and increased capital expenditures

** Jefferies says United Rentals' margin outlook was cut due to higher equipment spending and delivery costs

** Truist notes that co's profitability remains under pressure due to continued growth in lower-margin ancillary services

** Co on Wednesday missed Q3 profit estimates

** However, Citi raise its PT to $1,140 from $1,130, citing strong forward demand for rental products as a key driver of growth

** 15 of 23 brokerages rate the stock "buy" or higher, 7 "hold" and 1 "sell" ; their median PT is $1,022- data compiled by LSEG

data.title: Colombian man sentenced to 42 years for murder and dismemberment of UK couple
data.description:
A Colombian national who murdered two men in west London and transported parts of their dismembered bodies in suitcases to southwest England was sentenced on Friday to 42 years in prison.

Yostin Andres Mosquera, 35, was convicted in July of killing Albert Alfonso, 62, and Paul Longworth, 71, at their flat in July 2024. Prosecutors said Mosquera had bludgeoned Longworth with a hammer and stabbed Alfonso to death after the pair had sex.

He then decapitated and dismembered both victims, placing parts of their bodies into suitcases and travelling to Bristol, southwest England, where he abandoned the luggage on the Clifton Suspension Bridge.

He had pleaded not guilty to two counts of murder but admitted to the manslaughter of Alfonso, claiming he had lost control after Alfonso killed Longworth and threatened his family. The jury rejected his defence and found him guilty of both murders.

On Friday, Mosquera pleaded guilty to three new charges of possessing indecent images of children.

Judge Joel Bennathan, who described the murders as "thoroughly wicked crimes," handed down his sentence of life imprisonment with a minimum term of 42 years at Woolwich Crown Court on Friday.

data.title: U.S. Stocks at New Highs on Mild Inflation — Market Talk
data.description:
0955 ET - U.S. stock indexes open at record levels as a mild September inflation report fuels hopes of an extended decline in interest rates. The delayed CPI data is at or below expectations, allowing the Fed to proceed with its monetary easing. While the government shutdown raises doubts about the availability of data to guide policymakers, for now Wall Street is happy. The S&P 500 and DJIA rise 0.8% and the Nasdaq adds 1%, all on pace to set new record highs. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

data.title: U.K. to Reopen Sept 2038 Index-Linked Gilt Via Syndication in Week Starting Nov. 10
data.description:
By Miriam Mukuru

The U.K. Debt Management Office plans to reopen the September 2038 index-linked gilt through syndication in the week starting Nov. 10, subject to demand and market conditions, it said on Friday.

This will be the fifth syndication by the DMO in fiscal 2026. The DMO has raised a total of 32.4 billion pounds ($43.2 billion) through the previous four syndications in fiscal 2026, it said.

"It is expected that the upcoming syndication will be a modestly-sized transaction," the DMO said.

Write to Miriam Mukuru at miriam.mukuru@wsj.com

data.title: P&G Stock Rises on Earnings. Why Tariffs Are Still a Factor to Watch. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Nate Wolf

Shares of Procter & Gamble rose Friday after the maker of consumer products reported better-than-expected quarterly earnings.

The company posted core earnings of $1.99 a share for its fiscal first quarter, surpassing analysts' consensus estimate of $1.90. Sales totaled $22.4 billion, above Wall Street's call for $20.9 billion and up 3% from the prior year.

P&G stock was up 2.4% in premarket trading Friday. Shares have fallen 9.8% this year as of Thursday's close.

"These results keep us on track to deliver within our guidance ranges on all key financial metrics for the fiscal year in a challenging consumer and geopolitical environment," said CEO Jon Moeller in a statement.

P&G now expects to incur higher costs from tariffs of around $400 million after tax. However, the company reiterated its fiscal 2026 outlook for sales growth of between 1% and 5% and for core per-share earnings of $6.83 to $7.09.

Write to Nate Wolf at nate.wolf@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: US Composite PMI Rises to 3-Month High
data.description:
The S&P Global US Composite PMI rose to 54.8 in October 2025 from 53.9 in September, marking the highest reading since July and signaling faster growth slightly above the Q3 average.

Business activity expanded for the 33rd consecutive month, with overall growth in both manufacturing (PMI 52.2) and services (PMI 54.2).

October saw the strongest increase in new business so far in 2025, though exports continued to decline.

Manufacturers reported reduced input purchases and falling backlogs, leading to a record buildup of unsold inventories.

Employment growth improved modestly but remained subdued, particularly in manufacturing, as firms expressed weaker confidence amid policy uncertainty and tariff concerns.

Still, sentiment was supported by lower interest rates.

Output prices rose at the slowest pace since April, even as input costs continued to climb sharply due to tariffs and wage pressures, highlighting ongoing cost challenges despite resilient business activity.

data.title: US Manufacturing PMI Edges Higher in October
data.description:
The S&P Global US Manufacturing PMI rose to 52.2 in October 2025, up from 52.0 in September and slightly above expectations of 52.0, according to a preliminary estimate.

The reading marked an improvement in factory conditions for the ninth time in ten months, supported by accelerating production and the sharpest rise in new orders in 20 months.

However, employment growth eased to a three-month low, while input inventories increased only marginally.

The suppliers’ delivery times index contributed modestly to the headline PMI, reflecting a slower lengthening of lead times compared with September.

data.title: Organto Foods Announces Intention to Prepay Outstanding Debentures
data.description:
VANCOUVER, BC AND BREDA, NETHERLANDS / ACCESS Newswire / October 24, 2025 / Organto Foods Inc. (TSX-V:OGO)(OTCQB:OGOFF) ("Organto" or the "Company"), today announced that as part of its ongoing efforts to reduce debt and strengthen its financial position, the Company has elected to give notice of prepayment to the holders of its outstanding December 2024 series A, December 2024 series B, February 2025 and March 2025 series of convertible debentures in the aggregate principal amount of $2,340,850 outstanding as at October 23, 2025, bearing interest at the rate of 10% per annum (the "Convertible Debentures").

Pursuant to the terms of the Convertible Debentures, the Company may prepay the principal sum owing under the Convertible Debentures in whole or in part, together with any unpaid interest thereon calculated to the date of prepayment (the "Prepayment Date") without penalty by providing written notice (the "Prepayment Notice") to the holders of the Convertible Debentures (the "Holders") 30 days prior to the Prepayment Date, during which time the Holders may exercise their conversion rights in the manner set out in the certificates representing the Convertible Debentures.

The Company is delivering the Prepayment Notice to the Holders advising them that Friday, November 28, 2025, has been set as the Prepayment Date. Pursuant to the terms of the Convertible Debentures, Holders have the right to convert their Convertible Debentures (the "Conversion Right") into common shares of the Company ("Shares") at a conversion price of $0.60 per Share.

In the event that no Holders exercise their Conversion Right prior to the Prepayment Date, the Company expects that the aggregate cash payment to be made to Holders in respect of all outstanding Convertible Debentures will be $2,549,679, comprised of the principal sum totaling $2,340,850 and unpaid interest to the Prepayment date of totaling $206,829. In the event that all Holders exercise 100% of their Conversion Right prior to the Prepayment Date, the Company expects to issue an aggregate of 3,901,417 Shares and pay an aggregate cash payment in the amount of approximately $206,829 to Holders in respect of the conversion of the Convertible Debentures and payment in full of all unpaid interest.

ON BEHALF OF THE BOARD

Steve Bromley

Co-Chair and CEO

For more information, contact:

Investor Relations

John Rathwell, Senior Vice President Corporate Development

647 629 0018

info@organto.com

ABOUT ORGANTO

Organto is an integrated provider of branded, private label, and distributed organic, fairtrade, and non-GMO fruit and vegetable products using a strategic asset-lighter business model to serve a growing socially responsible and health-conscious consumer around the globe. Organto's business model is rooted in its commitment to sustainable business practices focused on environmental responsibility and a commitment to the communities where it operates, its people, and its shareholders.

FORWARD LOOKING STATEMENTS

This news release may include certain forward-looking information and statements, as defined by law, including without limitation, Canadian securities laws and the "safe harbor" provisions of the US Private Securities Litigation Reform Act ("forward-looking statements"). In particular, and without limitation, this news release contains forward-looking statements respecting the expected timing of the prepayment of the Convertible Debentures and the anticipated amount(s) of Shares, principal sum and interest to be issued and/or paid, as applicable upon the completion of the prepayment of the Convertible Debentures. Forward-looking statements are based on a number of assumptions that may prove to be incorrect, including, without limitation, the assumption that all applicable regulatory and/or other requisite approvals, if required, will be obtained in a timely manner and on acceptable terms; that the Company will have sufficient cash on hand to satisfy its prepayment obligations in full; and that all conditions precedent to the prepayment and/or conversion of the Convertible Debentures will be satisfied in a timely manner. There can be no assurance that forward-looking statements will prove to be accurate, as actual results and future events could differ materially from those anticipated in such statements. Accordingly, readers should not place undue reliance on forward-looking statements. Factors that could cause actual results to differ materially from those anticipated in forward-looking statements in this news release include, among others, regulatory risks including related to market volatility and economic conditions; risks related to unforeseen delays; and risks that necessary financing will be unavailable when needed. For further information on these and other risks and uncertainties that may affect the Company's business, see the "Risks and Uncertainties" and "Forward-Looking Statements" sections of the Company's annual and interim management's discussion and analysis filings with the Canadian securities regulators, which are available under the Company's profile at www.sedarplus.ca. Except as required by law, Organto does not assume any obligation to release publicly any revisions to forward-looking statements contained in this news release to reflect events or circumstances after the date hereof or to reflect the occurrence of unanticipated events.

Neither the TSX Venture Exchange nor its Regulation Services Provider (as that term is defined in the policies of the TSX Venture Exchange) accepts responsibility for the adequacy or accuracy of this news release.

data.title: Hilltop Holdings Inc reports results for the quarter ended September 30 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Hilltop Holdings Inc reported quarterly adjusted earnings of 74 cents​​ per share for the quarter ended September 30, higher than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of 46 cents. The mean expectation of four analysts for the quarter was for earnings of 49 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from 40 cents to 60 cents per share.

  • Revenue rose 7% to $112.39 million from a year ago; analysts expected $109.86 million.

  • Hilltop Holdings Inc's reported EPS for the quarter was 74 cents​.

  • The company reported quarterly net income of $45.82 million.

  • Hilltop Holdings Inc shares had fallen by 2.9% this quarter and gained 13.3% so far this year.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts was unchanged in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, there have been no negative revisions of earnings estimates

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "hold" and the breakdown of recommendations is no "strong buy" or "buy," 4 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell." The average consensus recommendation for the banks peer group is "buy."

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Hilltop Holdings Inc is $34.00, about 4.6% above its last closing price of $32.44

data.title: U.S. Stocks Open Higher After Inflation Report — WSJ
data.description:
By Caitlin McCabe

Investors welcomed softer-than-expected inflation data, with stock indexes rising and bond yields declining.

The consumer-price index for September, which was delayed due to the government shutdown, showed inflation running at 3% in the 12 months through September, a touch below consensus forecasts. Monthly price rises slowed to 0.3%, when they had been expected to hold steady at 0.4%.

Investors and policymakers have been deprived of macroeconomic data as the shutdown drags into a fourth week. Friday's CPI reinforced the conviction, already strong among investors, that the Federal Reserve is likely to cut rates by a standard quarter-point at its two remaining meetings this year.

Major U.S. stock indexes were on track to end the week with gains of 1% or more apiece. They also have been bolstered by a spate of strong earnings reports and optimism over news President Trump will meet with Chinese leader Xi Jinping next week.

Procter & Gamble, HCA Healthcare and General Dynamics reported results before the open, rounding out a busy earnings week. Results late Thursday from Intel boosted its stock.

In recent trading:

Oil prices ticked up, following the rally that came Thursday after President Trump imposed sanctions on two Russian oil giants. Brent crude futures rose above $66 a barrel.

The Canadian dollar slipped against the greenback, after President Trump said he was terminating bilateral trade talks.

Chinese chip stocks rallied on Beijing's new economic blueprint, which calls for China become more technologically self-sufficient over the next five years.

Write to Caitlin McCabe at caitlin.mccabe@wsj.com

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Sika's Operational Momentum is Overshadowed by China Weakness — Market Talk
data.description: Sika's healthy operational momentum was overshadowed by dragging foreign exchange rates and construction weakness in China, Vontobel's Alexander Koller says in a note. "We see limited topline upside near term, but margin protection remains best-in-class," Koller says. The Switzerland-based speciality-chemicals company could benefit from positive momentum in the coming months stemming from favorable impulses from the U.S economy, he says. While its full-year targets seem achievable, the reduction to the mid-term sales view is understandable against the still challenging construction environment, he adds. Shares trade 0.4% lower at 176.2 Swiss francs. (nina.kienle@wsj.com)
